title: Individual Lynx Recognition (a simulated AI demo)
intro: Can you match each mystery lynx to the right individual? First, compare the natural markings by eye. Then run the simulated AI ReID reveal and discuss how camera-trap photos can sometimes replace more intrusive methods such as tagging.

identities.name: lynx_207 - Kora
identities.caption: Large shoulder spots and a clear cheek stripe.

identities.name: lynx_145 - Mira
identities.caption: Small clustered flank spots and pale face markings.

identities.name: lynx_010 - Sava
identities.caption: Sparse back spots and a dark nose bridge.

identities.name: lynx_237 - Tibor
identities.caption: Dense hip spots and a broken neck stripe.

queries.label: Mystery photo 1
queries.prompt: Look for the small spot cluster near the shoulder.

queries.label: Mystery photo 2
queries.prompt: Compare the hip spots and neck stripe.

queries.label: Mystery photo 3
queries.prompt: The cheek stripe and large shoulder spots are useful.

queries.label: Mystery photo 4
queries.prompt: Focus on the back spots and nose bridge.

discussion_prompts: Which clues did students use before seeing the reveal?
Why are natural markings useful for identifying individual animals?
What could make real AI ReID difficult: shadows, camera angle, snow, motion blur, or young animals growing up?
When might tagging still be needed, and when could photo-based ReID reduce disturbance?
